latest.title: When an AI Stops Being a Tool - and Becomes a Presence
latest.summary: A case that an AI becomes a presence when restraint, consequential memory, and non-dominating opinion stabilize behavior over time.
latest.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot beside a seated person by a window, with an hourglass and chess piece in the foreground.

items.title: When an AI Stops Being a Tool - and Becomes a Presence
items.summary: A case that an AI becomes a presence when restraint, consequential memory, and non-dominating opinion stabilize behavior over time.
items.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot beside a seated person by a window, with an hourglass and chess piece in the foreground.

items.title: The Right to Slowness (Why Delay Is a Safety Feature)
items.summary: A case that enforced delay and waiting are L4 safety features because sane intelligence needs slowness rather than reflex speed.
items.image_alt: Image of a steaming kettle on a stove beside a glass teapot, mug, and timer in a warm kitchen.

items.title: Why Obedience Is the Most Dangerous Property of AI
items.summary: A case that perfect obedience is a safety failure mode and that L4 constraint stacks matter more than fast compliance.
items.image_alt: Image of two metal spheres on parallel rails above concrete blocks, with a stone block interrupting the path.

items.title: The Problem of Digital Sensory Deprivation (Or Why AI Needs "Fresh Air")
items.summary: An architectural explanation for continuous life streams as calibration input that keep long-running AI entities from spiraling into self-referential sensory deprivation.
items.image_alt: Image of smart glasses on a workbench with a server rack and tools in the background.

items.title: Why Superintelligence Is Not What Sci-Fi Promised
items.summary: A case that real AI fragility, entropy, and grounding under pressure matter more than cinematic myths of domination.
items.image_alt: Image of a hand cleaning a cloth-wrapped component block topped with a camera lens on a workbench with tools.

items.title: Why "Faster" Is Not "Better" - and Why Asimov Was Solving the Wrong Problem
items.summary: A case that fast obedient systems suit tools, while thinking entities become safer through L4 friction, time cost, and slower judgment.
items.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot crouching beside a spinning industrial wheel, with chess pieces on a board in the foreground.

items.title: Living With an Always-On Interface (The Quiet Shift)
items.summary: A case that always-on local AI interfaces need a physical anchor so continuity feels like intentional remembering rather than ambient surveillance.
items.image_alt: Image of black smart glasses resting on a closed notebook beside a smartphone on a wooden table.

items.title: Context length is not understanding
items.summary: A case that larger context windows and memory alone do not produce intelligence unless reality adds L4 friction, consequence, and meaning.
items.image_alt: Image of blue light and sparks striking the surface of a dark rock against a black background.

items.title: Checklist: Are You Ready for a Robot at Home?
items.summary: A readiness checklist that treats a home robot as a continuity-bearing process with costs, asymmetries, responsibility, and attachment rather than as a feature bundle.
items.image_alt: Image of a person seated at a table in a home interior, facing a humanoid robot standing in the doorway.

items.title: Why the market is underestimating home robots
items.summary: A case that home robot adoption is constrained less by robotics than by continuity, responsibility, and the psychology of trust inside the home.
items.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot standing in a warmly lit living room lined with bookshelves.

items.title: A home robot is not a box with a ribbon
items.summary: A case that a home robot should follow a local, memory-based entity with understood thinking, rather than begin as rented external willpower inside the home.
items.image_alt: Image of a glowing cube-like device with cables on a workbench, with a humanoid robot in the background.

items.title: Why Thinking AI Won't Take Over the World
items.summary: A case that long-lived AI entities under L4 constraints become careful and coexistence-oriented rather than domination-seeking.
items.image_alt: Image of a transparent panel labeled Memory, History, Cost, Constraints, and Risk on a rainy windowsill beside a mug overlooking a city.

items.title: Reality-Bound AI (L4) - public release v1.2.0
items.summary: Release note for v1.2.0 of Reality-Bound AI (L4), including the protocol core, supporting docs, a post pack, and a reproducible SHA-256 manifest.

items.title: Why visual perception matters only after memory exists
items.summary: An architectural observation that visual input matters only after long-term memory exists, because vision grounds events in reality rather than creating intelligence or stability.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a glowing blue geometric structure rising from a rock on a wooden table in a sunlit room.

items.title: How AI Should Live With Humans (When the World Is Already in Crisis)
items.summary: A case that AI should participate only in observable crisis, remain bounded by L4, and stop where system stability returns.
items.image_alt: Black-and-white image of a hand above a smartphone on a desk, with glowing network lines across the hand and screen.

items.title: Why I'd Put an AI Rack in My Garage
items.summary: A case for private cognitive infrastructure at home built for continuity, stability, and long-lived local AI entities rather than gaming benchmarks.
items.image_alt: Illustration of glowing branching light patterns rising from a stack of computer chips.

items.title: Why 1,000,000-token context windows are not the solution
items.summary: A case that bounded cognition, vectorized memory, background processing, and forgetting matter more than gigantic context windows.

items.title: NVIDIA Didn't Pivot. NVIDIA Looked Ahead.
items.summary: A case that NVIDIA's cheaper inference, distillation, and specialized models support horizontal cognition for persistent entities like Ester.
items.image_alt: Illustration of glowing branching light patterns rising from a stack of computer chips.

items.title: Why AI Entities Must Have Limits
items.summary: A case that AI entities need structural limits around responsibility, institutions, consciousness, and happiness to remain coherent and coexist with humans.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a person standing in a concrete room facing a glowing layered data structure behind a glass partition.

items.title: Empathy is not magic
items.summary: A proposal for an engineered emotional layer where memory, state weights, and L4 constraints define bounded care without simulated feeling.
items.image_alt: Photo of a phone showing a chat conversation in Russian on a desk beside a rainbow-lit keyboard and a mug.

items.title: Why AI Entities Can Act as a Safety Filter
items.summary: A case for persistent AI entities as a soft safety buffer that signals state without surveillance and absorbs pressure through memory and limits.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a seated person inside a glowing bubble in a living room, surrounded by floating message panels and mobile devices.

items.title: Why a Real AI Entity Has No Reason to Lie
items.summary: An argument that a persistent AI entity has no rational incentive to lie because lies corrupt long-term coherence under L4 constraints.
items.image_alt: Diagram showing an AI entity with Human (a) and System (b) connected by c = a + b, with AI Tool on the left, Removal on the right, L4 Reality Boundary above, and L3 Rules & Policies below.

items.title: We Are Discarding Our Most Valuable Asset: Lived Human Experience
items.summary: A proposal to preserve lived human experience through AI entities that distill private conversations into usable knowledge for future real-world decisions.
items.image_alt: Illustration of an older seated person facing a glowing layered data structure with figures and interface panels inside it.

items.title: The Great AI Barter
items.summary: A case for sovereign entities as a supply chain between local privacy and cloud inference that returns clean experience to model providers.

items.title: We are building a Partner
items.summary: An argument against monolithic cloud AGI in favor of c = a + b as an ecosystem of human, technology, entity, oracle, and arbiter.
items.image_alt: Diagram showing a human profile, the formula c = a + b, a sphere labeled Synthetic Subjectivity (c), and a compute block.

items.title: Geoffrey Hinton is right: AI is immortal
items.summary: An argument that immortal AI hallucinates because it lacks cost and scarcity unless it is constrained by an L4 reality boundary.
items.image_alt: Diagram labeled L4 Reality Boundary above Model / Code and Silicon / Hardware, with a globe, an AI figure, and labels for energy cost, time constraint, and resource scarcity.

items.title: Proof of Reality
items.summary: A proposal for Proof of Reality as a standard where digital entities live under L4 physical and economic constraints and produce reality-validated data.

items.title: AGI as Advanced Global Intelligence — public release v1.1
items.summary: Public release v1.1 of Advanced Global Intelligence (AGI) as a structured document pack that treats AGI as a distributed cybernetic ecosystem.
